nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

nodes.name: 缅甸
nodes.summary: 緬甸聯邦共和國（緬甸語：ပြည်ထောင်စု သမ္မတ မြန်မာနိုင်ငံတော်，羅馬化：Pranyhtaungcu. Sa.ma.ta.

nodes.name: 泰国
nodes.summary: 泰国是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联251种鸟类，包括青头潜鸭、銹紅林鷓鴣、环颈山鹧鸪等251种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 溫帶落葉林
nodes.summary: 溫帶落葉林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括Jandaya parakeet、栗臀䴓，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 常綠森林
nodes.summary: 常綠森林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联442种鸟类，包括紫冠雉、角冠雉、Razor-billed curassow等442种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 栗臀䴓
nodes.summary: 栗臀䴓（Chestnut-vented nuthatch，学名 Sitta nagaensis），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、气候变化。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Chestnut-vented nuthatches are found in the northeast of India, in parts of Tibet and south-central China, descending into eastern Myanmar and northwestern Thailand

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Chestnut-vented nuthatches are found in the northeast of India, in parts of Tibet and south-central China, descending into eastern Myanmar and northwestern Thailand

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |name-list-style=amp |date=1998 |url=https://static1.squarespace.com/static/5c1a9e03f407b482a158da87/t/5c1fbe3888251bb6cac14f37/1545584194551/Duckworth-Laos.pdf |title=Bird records from Laos, October 1994-August 1995 |journal=Forktail |volume=13 |pages=33–68}} The chestnut-vented nuthatch usually inhabits evergreen forests of the uplands, or forests and pine patches growing on dry ridges in the middle of evergreen forests

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Locally, it also occurs in deciduous forests in northeastern India, in Quercus subsericea oak forests and alder groves in northeastern Myanmar, spruce forests (Picea sp.), fir forests (Abies sp.) or rhododendron stands in Yunnan, or poplar (Populus sp.) and walnut (Juglans sp.) in Sichuan. The altitudinal distribution ranges from in India, Thailand, and Myanmar, occasionally occurring up to in the latter

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The population of the chestnut-vented nuthatch is declining due to habitat destruction and fragmentation, but its range is large and was estimated to be 3.8 million km2 (1.5 million square miles), and the species is generally common throughout its range. For these reasons, the chestnut-vented nuthatch is considered to be of least concern by the International Union for the Conservation of Nature. A study carried out in 2009 tried to predict the impact of climate change on the distribution of several species of nuthatches in Asia by modeling two scenarios

For these reasons, the chestnut-vented nuthatch is considered to be of least concern by the International Union for the Conservation of Nature.
